title: hebe dasyphylla
intro: Hebe dasyphylla, also known as the dwarf alpine hebe or dwarf green hebe, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. Native to New Zealand, this shrubby perennial plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The leaves and flowers of Hebe dasyphylla have been used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which are said to offer health benefits like reduced stress, improved digestion, and immune system support. As a herbal remedy, Hebe dasyphylla is often used to treat various health issues, including colds, fever, and respiratory problems, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
